session_summary: An 8-hour session that designed the main manuscript's organization (full rewrite off the old spectral framing, derivation.qmd kept as a separate appendix-level proof) and drafted the front matter, a provenance/verification section, and sections 3-7 of mi-spectral.qmd. Marcus set the strategic frame (make the AI-derivation provenance visible to a skeptical reviewer, include Monte Carlo and honest failed predictions), reviewed each drafted chunk on the console, and through a confused probe about the Satorra-Bentler correction surfaced a real bug: the IC simulation applied SB as a standalone substitute rather than wrapping the correction, which he then directed be flagged in the draft, filed as a git issue (#1), noted in CLAUDE.md, and committed/pushed.

coded_turns.quote: Now let’s talk about the manuscript organization etc. the derivation.qmd is the appendix level proof
coded_turns.note: Opens manuscript-organization work and fixes derivation.qmd's role as the appendix-level proof.

coded_turns.quote: 1. Complete rewrite. 2. Keep separate
coded_turns.note: Picks clean rewrite of the stub and a separate derivation document from the offered options.

coded_turns.quote: Keep separate for now, eventually we’ll put in our manuscript qmd
coded_turns.note: Restates: full rewrite, derivation stays separate now but folds into the manuscript later.

coded_turns.quote: I want be attuned to a reviewer who rightfully might wonder: how do I know this isn’t just AI slop
coded_turns.note: Frames the core review risk; demands a provenance/triangulation section to make the invisible verification visible.

coded_turns.quote: We also have Monte Carlo simulation results
coded_turns.note: Adds Monte Carlo evidence as a triangulation leg the AI had omitted from the verification plan.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: manuscript section 2 / W1-W3 MC as verification pillar

coded_turns.quote: Yes let’s do it
coded_turns.note: Approves the pre-registration-and-honest-scoreboard MC framing and drafting the first chunk.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: manuscript front matter + Intro + Verification draft

coded_turns.quote: Do you need to enter plan mode?
coded_turns.note: Checks the operating mode before the AI starts writing; light process nudge.

coded_turns.quote: Can you out out here. I’m in bed on my phone
coded_turns.note: Pauses the work; constrains interaction to phone-friendly console review.

coded_turns.quote: I meant can you show me the text on the console
coded_turns.note: Clarifies he wants drafted prose shown inline rather than committed, to read on phone.

coded_turns.quote: This looks fine for now. Let’s continue
coded_turns.note: Accepts the front matter / Intro / Verification draft and directs continuing to next sections.

coded_turns.quote: Show me the new sections on the console
coded_turns.note: Asks to inspect freshly drafted sections inline before proceeding.

coded_turns.quote: Let’s continue
coded_turns.note: Approves sections 3-4 and directs drafting the application sections.

coded_turns.quote: Show me here
coded_turns.note: Requests inline display of the just-drafted application sections.

coded_turns.quote: Ok. Write discussion
coded_turns.note: Directs drafting of the section 7 Discussion, the last stub.

coded_turns.quote: Did we ever talk about the Satorra Bentler correction?
coded_turns.note: Raises SB correction as a missing menu item, surfacing a domain reference the draft had not placed.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: SB menu in sections 5-6 / todo/003 reference

coded_turns.quote: How did of perform in simulations with our bias correction. Remember Chan did not  correct bias he did his simulations
coded_turns.note: Probes whether SB was simulated with the bias correction; reminds that Chan never corrected the bias.

coded_turns.quote: I thought the bias correction was always applied with the SB
coded_turns.note: Voices a mismatch between his mental model and the code, pressing on whether SB wraps the correction.

coded_turns.quote: I meant the IC simulation to do this: You meant SB to always wrap the correction
coded_turns.note: Pins the bug: the IC simulation's SB rule should wrap the correction, not be a standalone substitute.

coded_turns.quote: Let’s flag in the draft, write a git issue, and mention the outstanding issue in CLAUDE or something so we know to ret
coded_turns.note: Imposes a three-point tracking protocol for the open bug: draft flag, git issue, CLAUDE.md note.

coded_turns.quote: Go ahead and push
coded_turns.note: Authorizes committing the rewrite plus pointers; greenlights the push.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: commit of mi-spectral.qmd rewrite + todo/000 + CLAUDE.md

coded_turns.quote: Go ahead and push it
coded_turns.note: Confirms pushing the commit to origin after the AI flagged a piggybacked earlier commit.

ai_side.evidence: do you want me to treat `mi-spectral.qmd` as a clean rewrite ... or are there pieces of that old structure you still want to salvage?
ai_side.note: Put the stub-handling and appendix-mechanics choices to Marcus rather than deciding.

ai_side.evidence: front section (visible to skeptic immediately, my default) vs. a prominent appendix the Intro points to ... I lean front
ai_side.note: Laid out placement options for the verification section with a stated lean.

ai_side.evidence: §6's framing sentence says "MI-AIC, MI-BIC" (matching the abstract), but the empirics are AIC-based selection only ... just flagging it
ai_side.note: Self-flagged an abstract-vs-empirics mismatch in section 6.

ai_side.evidence: 1. **It's deliberate** ... 2. **You meant SB to always wrap the correction** ... Which is it?
ai_side.note: Posed the two SB interpretations as a forced choice to resolve the bug.

ai_side.evidence: 1. **Make the edit + re-run W3 now** ... 2. **Make the edit but hold the re-run** ... 3. **Hold entirely**
ai_side.note: Offered three remediation paths and refused to run compute or touch frozen files without say-so.
